LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. LIBERTY IS A PRIVATE PARTY, NOT A GOVERNMENTAL AGENCY, AND THE PREFILING REQUIREMENT THAT IT SEEKS TO IMPOSE ON PLAINTIFF IS TO BE DETERMINED BY LIBERTY ITSELF, NOT THE UC OR ANY OTHER GOVERNMENT, SO THE DOCTRINE OF EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES IS INAPPLICABLE. WHILE A PRIVATE PARTY CONTRACT CAN IMPOSE A PREFILING REQUIREMENT, THE LANGUAGE OF THE PARTIES' CONTRACT SHOWS THAT AN APPEAL OF UC REGULATIONS IS PERMISSIVE, NOT OBLIGATORY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185; DEPT. 302. =302/HK

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL J. VEROFF OF PILLSBURY/COLEMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 433-8000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT FORNI OF ROPERS MAJESKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 364-8280 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LIBERTY LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF BOSTON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled with ten days to answer. Liberty is a private party, not a governmental agency, and the prefiling requirement that it seeks to impose on plaintiff is to be determined by Liberty itself, not the UC or any other government, so the doctrine of exhaustion of administrative remedies is inapplicable. While a private party contract can impose a prefiling requirement, the language of the parties' contract shows that an appeal of UC regulations is permissive, not obligatory. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk
